markdownRemark.html:
A collection of interesting things I’ve discovered this month:

  • NetArchTest - “A fluent API for .NET Standard that can enforce architectural rules in unit tests.”
  • Writing Logs to XUnit Test Output
  • ASP.NET Core Application Part For Testing Anti-forgery Protected Resources
  • When ASP.NET Core can’t find your controller: debugging application parts
  • Moq.AutoMock
  • Snitch - “A tool that help you find transitive (nuget) package references that can be removed”
  • Atlassian JSON Schema Viewer
  • Accidental DBA
  • Current Status of Python Packaging - 2019
  • Poetry - “Poetry is a tool for dependency management and packaging in Python. It allows you to declare the libraries your project depends on and it will manage (install/update) them for you.”
  • Tilehurst Memories
  • NuxtJS
  • Refit
  • you can book me
  • NAudio 2 plans
  • Logical Increments
  • AsyncFixer
  • Create a Radius Map - “Get the miles radius or km radius distance from any location”
  • Rxmarbles - “Interactive diagrams of Rx Observables”
  • Domainr
  • Apache JMeter
  • CSS Utility Classes and “Separation of Concerns”
  • explainshell.com
  • wide·screen gam·ing fo·rum (wsgf)
  • json:api
  • lossless-cut - “The swiss army knife of lossless video/audio editing”
  • JavaScript Array Explorer - “Find the array method you need without digging through the docs”
  • WebSequenceDiagrams
  • Reverse Package Search
  • C# Pad
